query: We merged record A with record B, then B with C. But A and C are still flagged as duplicates in our review queue afterward. Why is this happening and how do I fix it?
expected_behavior: Identifies the failure mode as transitive-collapse failure caused by greedy pairwise merging
Recommends rebuilding the duplicate graph and running union-find / connected-components on the full set of edges at once, not pairwise in passes
Names index-refresh between passes (or single-pass union-find) as the specific remediation — greedy pairwise merging with stale canonical-id indices is the root cause
